title: luzula spicata subsp. saximontana
intro: Luzula spicata subsp. saximontana, also known as Saxifrage Woodrush, is a subspecies of the Luzula spicata plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Saxifrage Woodrush include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and aid in wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion for its medicinal purposes, and in some cases, it is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to aid in skin and wound healing.
